databricks_catalog.browse_only: Indicates whether the principal is limited to retrieving metadata for the associated object through the BROWSE privilege when include_browse is enabled in the request.
databricks_catalog.connection_name: The name of the connection to an external data source.
databricks_catalog.created_at: Time at which this catalog was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_catalog.enable_predictive_optimization: Whether predictive optimization should be enabled for this object and objects under it.
databricks_catalog.full_name: The full name of the catalog. Corresponds with the name field.
databricks_catalog.isolation_mode: Whether the current securable is accessible from all workspaces or a specific set of workspaces.
databricks_catalog.managed_encryption_settings: Control CMK encryption for managed catalog data
databricks_catalog.name: Name of catalog.
databricks_catalog.options: A map of key-value properties attached to the securable.
databricks_catalog.owner: Username of current owner of catalog.
databricks_catalog.properties: A map of key-value properties attached to the securable.
databricks_catalog.provider_name: The name of delta sharing provider.
databricks_catalog.share_name: The name of the share under the share provider.
databricks_catalog.storage_location: Storage Location URL (full path) for managed tables within catalog.
databricks_catalog.storage_root: Storage root URL for managed tables within catalog.
databricks_catalog.updated_at: Time at which this catalog was last modified, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_catalog.updated_by: Username of user who last modified catalog.

databricks_cluster.apply_policy_default_values: When set to true, fixed and default values from the policy will be used for fields that are omitted. When set to false, only fixed values from the policy will be applied.
databricks_cluster.autoscale: Parameters needed in order to automatically scale clusters up and down based on load. Note: autoscaling works best with DB runtime versions 3.0 or later.
databricks_cluster.autotermination_minutes: Automatically terminates the cluster after it is inactive for this time in minutes. If not set, this cluster will not be automatically terminated. If specified, the threshold must be between 10 and 10000 minutes. Users can also set this value to 0 to explicitly disable automatic termination.
databricks_cluster.aws_attributes: Attributes related to clusters running on Amazon Web Services. If not specified at cluster creation, a set of default values will be used.
databricks_cluster.azure_attributes: Attributes related to clusters running on Microsoft Azure. If not specified at cluster creation, a set of default values will be used.
databricks_cluster.clone_from: When specified, this clones libraries from a source cluster during the creation of a new cluster.
databricks_cluster.cluster_cores: Number of CPU cores available for this cluster. Note that this can be fractional, e.g. 7.5 cores, since certain node types are configured to share cores between Spark nodes on the same instance.
databricks_cluster.cluster_id: Canonical identifier for the cluster. This id is retained during cluster restarts and resizes, while each new cluster has a globally unique id.
databricks_cluster.cluster_log_conf: The configuration for delivering spark logs to a long-term storage destination. Three kinds of destinations (DBFS, S3 and Unity Catalog volumes) are supported. Only one destination can be specified for one cluster. If the conf is given, the logs will be delivered to the destination every `5 mins`. The destination of driver logs is `$destination/$clusterId/driver`, while the destination of executor logs is `$destination/$clusterId/executor`.
databricks_cluster.cluster_memory_mb: Total amount of cluster memory, in megabytes
databricks_cluster.cluster_name: Cluster name requested by the user. This doesn't have to be unique. If not specified at creation, the cluster name will be an empty string. For job clusters, the cluster name is automatically set based on the job and job run IDs.
databricks_cluster.cluster_source: Determines whether the cluster was created by a user through the UI, created by the Databricks Jobs Scheduler, or through an API request.
databricks_cluster.creator_user_name: Creator user name. The field won't be included in the response if the user has already been deleted.
databricks_cluster.custom_tags: Additional tags for cluster resources. Databricks will tag all cluster resources (e.g., AWS instances and EBS volumes) with these tags in addition to `default_tags`. Notes:
databricks_cluster.default_tags: Tags that are added by Databricks regardless of any `custom_tags`, including:
databricks_cluster.driver: Node on which the Spark driver resides. The driver node contains the Spark master and the Databricks application that manages the per-notebook Spark REPLs.
databricks_cluster.driver_instance_pool_id: The optional ID of the instance pool for the driver of the cluster belongs. The pool cluster uses the instance pool with id (instance_pool_id) if the driver pool is not assigned.
databricks_cluster.driver_node_type_flexibility: Flexible node type configuration for the driver node.
databricks_cluster.driver_node_type_id: The node type of the Spark driver. Note that this field is optional; if unset, the driver node type will be set as the same value as `node_type_id` defined above.
databricks_cluster.enable_elastic_disk: Autoscaling Local Storage: when enabled, this cluster will dynamically acquire additional disk space when its Spark workers are running low on disk space.
databricks_cluster.enable_local_disk_encryption: Whether to enable LUKS on cluster VMs' local disks
databricks_cluster.executors: Nodes on which the Spark executors reside.
databricks_cluster.gcp_attributes: Attributes related to clusters running on Google Cloud Platform. If not specified at cluster creation, a set of default values will be used.
databricks_cluster.init_scripts: The configuration for storing init scripts. Any number of destinations can be specified. The scripts are executed sequentially in the order provided. If `cluster_log_conf` is specified, init script logs are sent to `<destination>/<cluster-ID>/init_scripts`.
databricks_cluster.instance_pool_id: The optional ID of the instance pool to which the cluster belongs.
databricks_cluster.is_single_node: This field can only be used when `kind = CLASSIC_PREVIEW`.
databricks_cluster.jdbc_port: Port on which Spark JDBC server is listening, in the driver nod. No service will be listeningon on this port in executor nodes.
databricks_cluster.last_restarted_time: the timestamp that the cluster was started/restarted
databricks_cluster.last_state_loss_time: Time when the cluster driver last lost its state (due to a restart or driver failure).
databricks_cluster.node_type_id: This field encodes, through a single value, the resources available to each of the Spark nodes in this cluster. For example, the Spark nodes can be provisioned and optimized for memory or compute intensive workloads. A list of available node types can be retrieved by using the :method:clusters/listNodeTypes API call.
databricks_cluster.num_workers: Number of worker nodes that this cluster should have. A cluster has one Spark Driver and `num_workers` Executors for a total of `num_workers` + 1 Spark nodes.
databricks_cluster.policy_id: The ID of the cluster policy used to create the cluster if applicable.
databricks_cluster.remote_disk_throughput: If set, what the configurable throughput (in Mb/s) for the remote disk is. Currently only supported for GCP HYPERDISK_BALANCED disks.
databricks_cluster.runtime_engine: Determines the cluster's runtime engine, either standard or Photon.
databricks_cluster.single_user_name: Single user name if data_security_mode is `SINGLE_USER`
databricks_cluster.spark_conf: An object containing a set of optional, user-specified Spark configuration key-value pairs. Users can also pass in a string of extra JVM options to the driver and the executors via `spark.driver.extraJavaOptions` and `spark.executor.extraJavaOptions` respectively.
databricks_cluster.spark_context_id: A canonical SparkContext identifier. This value *does* change when the Spark driver restarts. The pair `(cluster_id, spark_context_id)` is a globally unique identifier over all Spark contexts.
databricks_cluster.spark_env_vars: An object containing a set of optional, user-specified environment variable key-value pairs. Please note that key-value pair of the form (X,Y) will be exported as is (i.e., `export X='Y'`) while launching the driver and workers.
databricks_cluster.spark_version: The Spark version of the cluster, e.g. `3.3.x-scala2.11`. A list of available Spark versions can be retrieved by using the :method:clusters/sparkVersions API call.
databricks_cluster.spec: The spec contains a snapshot of the latest user specified settings that were used to create/edit the cluster. Note: not included in the response of the ListClusters API.
databricks_cluster.ssh_public_keys: SSH public key contents that will be added to each Spark node in this cluster. The corresponding private keys can be used to login with the user name `ubuntu` on port `2200`. Up to 10 keys can be specified.
databricks_cluster.start_time: Time (in epoch milliseconds) when the cluster creation request was received (when the cluster entered a `PENDING` state).
databricks_cluster.state_message: A message associated with the most recent state transition (e.g., the reason why the cluster entered a `TERMINATED` state).
databricks_cluster.terminated_time: Time (in epoch milliseconds) when the cluster was terminated, if applicable.
databricks_cluster.termination_reason: Information about why the cluster was terminated. This field only appears when the cluster is in a `TERMINATING` or `TERMINATED` state.
databricks_cluster.total_initial_remote_disk_size: If set, what the total initial volume size (in GB) of the remote disks should be. Currently only supported for GCP HYPERDISK_BALANCED disks.
databricks_cluster.use_ml_runtime: This field can only be used when `kind = CLASSIC_PREVIEW`.
databricks_cluster.worker_node_type_flexibility: Flexible node type configuration for worker nodes.

databricks_metastore.cloud: Cloud vendor of the metastore home shard (e.g., `aws`, `azure`, `gcp`).
databricks_metastore.created_at: Time at which this metastore was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_metastore.default_data_access_config_id: Unique identifier of the metastore's (Default) Data Access Configuration.
databricks_metastore.delta_sharing_organization_name: The organization name of a Delta Sharing entity, to be used in Databricks-to-Databricks Delta Sharing as the official name.
databricks_metastore.delta_sharing_recipient_token_lifetime_i: The lifetime of delta sharing recipient token in seconds.
databricks_metastore.delta_sharing_scope: The scope of Delta Sharing enabled for the metastore.
databricks_metastore.external_access_enabled: Whether to allow non-DBR clients to directly access entities under the metastore.
databricks_metastore.global_metastore_id: Globally unique metastore ID across clouds and regions, of the form `cloud:region:metastore_id`.
databricks_metastore.name: The user-specified name of the metastore.
databricks_metastore.privilege_model_version: Privilege model version of the metastore, of the form `major.minor` (e.g., `1.0`).
databricks_metastore.region: Cloud region which the metastore serves (e.g., `us-west-2`, `westus`).
databricks_metastore.storage_root: The storage root URL for metastore
databricks_metastore.storage_root_credential_id: UUID of storage credential to access the metastore storage_root.
databricks_metastore.storage_root_credential_name: Name of the storage credential to access the metastore storage_root.
databricks_metastore.updated_at: Time at which the metastore was last modified, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_metastore.updated_by: Username of user who last modified the metastore.

databricks_schema.browse_only: Indicates whether the principal is limited to retrieving metadata for the associated object through the BROWSE privilege when include_browse is enabled in the request.
databricks_schema.catalog_type: The type of the parent catalog.
databricks_schema.created_at: Time at which this schema was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_schema.enable_predictive_optimization: Whether predictive optimization should be enabled for this object and objects under it.
databricks_schema.full_name: Full name of schema, in form of __catalog_name__.__schema_name__.
databricks_schema.name: Name of schema, relative to parent catalog.
databricks_schema.owner: Username of current owner of schema.
databricks_schema.properties: A map of key-value properties attached to the securable.
databricks_schema.schema_id: The unique identifier of the schema.
databricks_schema.storage_location: Storage location for managed tables within schema.
databricks_schema.storage_root: Storage root URL for managed tables within schema.
databricks_schema.updated_at: Time at which this schema was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_schema.updated_by: Username of user who last modified schema.

databricks_volume.access_point: The AWS access point to use when accesing s3 for this external location.
databricks_volume.browse_only: Indicates whether the principal is limited to retrieving metadata for the associated object through the BROWSE privilege when include_browse is enabled in the request.
databricks_volume.catalog_name: The name of the catalog where the schema and the volume are
databricks_volume.comment: The comment attached to the volume
databricks_volume.created_by: The identifier of the user who created the volume
databricks_volume.full_name: The three-level (fully qualified) name of the volume
databricks_volume.metastore_id: The unique identifier of the metastore
databricks_volume.name: The name of the volume
databricks_volume.owner: The identifier of the user who owns the volume
databricks_volume.schema_name: The name of the schema where the volume is
databricks_volume.storage_location: The storage location on the cloud
databricks_volume.updated_by: The identifier of the user who updated the volume last time
databricks_volume.volume_id: The unique identifier of the volume
databricks_volume.volume_type: The type of the volume. An external volume is located in the specified external location. A managed volume is located in the default location which is specified by the parent schema, or the parent catalog, or the Metastore. [Learn more]
